question.text: Which of the following entities CANNOT make a nomination for a demat account?

answer.text: An individual holding the account singly
Joint holders of an account (all signing together)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Non-individuals including society, trust, body corporate, HUF, and PoA holders cannot nominate. Only individuals (single or joint holders) can nominate. **The Trap:** 'A minor through a guardian' CAN nominate — minors are not barred from nominating if they act through a guardian. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers nomination rules for mutual fund investments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The logic: nomination applies on death of the holder, and only natural persons die. HUFs, trusts, and companies don't 'die' — they get partitioned, dissolved, or wound up.
